site: devmark / MDTool — client-side HTML→Markdown converter (no upload, no signup; built on turndown + GFM plugin)

pillar.title: HTML to Markdown Converter
pillar.notes: Existing app route (app/html-to-markdown) already has a strong 'How Ambiguous HTML Gets Resolved' section with an HTML-element-to-Markdown mapping table and 5 FAQs — stronger than most competitor tool pages. Same gap as the sibling pillars: nothing links to it from blog content yet.

clusters.name: Conversion Problems & Fixes
clusters.rationale: Verified via WebSearch: 'html to markdown code block not converting' returns Daring Fireball, Markdown Guide, and PHP-league/Turndown GitHub issue threads — zero overlap with the converter-tool SERP. Search also surfaced a real, citable failure mode (HTML in ISO-8859-1/Windows-1252 encoding produces garbled characters after conversion) and the lossy-by-design framing (Markdown ~15 constructs vs. HTML 100+ elements) that other competitor pages don't explain. High intent: visitor's output already looks wrong.

posts.title: Why Code Blocks Lose Their Language Tag When You Convert HTML to Markdown

posts.title: Garbled Characters After Converting HTML to Markdown? Fix the Encoding
posts.keyword: html to markdown encoding garbled characters

clusters.name: Comparisons & Tools
clusters.rationale: Verified via WebSearch: 'html to markdown converter', 'html to markdown online free', and 'best html to markdown converter 2026' all return 7+ shared results — codebeautify.org, htmlmarkdown.com, Syncfusion, Browserling, CodeShack, CloudConvert, PicoToolkit, Dillinger. Unlike the sibling 'Markdown to HTML' cluster, developer-facing library results (Turndown, JohannesKaufmann/html-to-markdown, the PyPI html-to-markdown package) rank inside this same head-term SERP rather than a separate one, so the comparison post should acknowledge the library option for scripted workflows while still routing manual/one-off conversions to the tool.

posts.title: Best HTML to Markdown Converter in 2026: DevMark vs. The Alternatives
Convert HTML to Markdown Without Uploading Your File
posts.keyword: html to markdown converter free no upload

clusters.name: AI & LLM Workflows
clusters.rationale: Verified via WebSearch: 'scrape website to markdown tool' returns an entirely separate, fast-moving competitor set (Firecrawl, Simplescraper, Bright Data, Decodo) built around full-page URL scraping for LLM ingestion — zero overlap with the static-HTML converter SERP. DevMark's tool is paste/upload-based (you already have the HTML — from view-source, a clipboard grab, an exported doc) rather than a URL fetcher, so this cluster deliberately targets the narrower 'I already have HTML, need clean Markdown for a prompt' job rather than competing with full-site crawlers head-on.

posts.title: How to Convert HTML to Markdown for ChatGPT and Claude Prompts
posts.keyword: convert html to markdown for llm prompt

posts.title: HTML to Markdown vs. Web Scraping Tools: When You Already Have the HTML
posts.keyword: html to markdown vs web scraper

clusters.name: Migration & Docs Workflows
clusters.rationale: Verified via WebSearch: 'convert confluence page to markdown' returns Atlassian Marketplace apps and GitHub exporters, and 'html email to markdown' returns file2markdown, Markdown Here, and PHP-league/html-to-markdown — each its own distinct competitor set with no overlap with the generic converter SERP. Same job-to-be-done pattern as the sibling clusters' CMS/dev-workflow buckets: a specific export format, a specific destination.

clusters.name: Fundamentals & Reference
